name: Stug
description: Firing a sticky, toxic, explosive compound, the Stug Gel Gun offers multiple ejection modes, delivering maximum damage in all situations.

components.name: Alloy Plate
components.description:
Carbon steel plates used to reinforce Grineer armor.

Location: Venus, Phobos, Ceres, Jupiter, Pluto, and Sedna.

components.name: Blueprint
components.description: Firing a sticky, toxic, explosive compound, the Stug Gel Gun offers multiple ejection modes, delivering maximum damage in all situations.

components.name: Gallium
components.description:
Soft metal used in microelectronics and energy weapons.

Location: Mars and Uranus

components.name: Neurodes
components.description:
Biotech sensor organ harvested from Infested entities.

components.name: Salvage

High value metals collected from war salvage.

Location: Mars, Jupiter, and Sedna

patchlogs.name: Nidus Prime & Plague Star: Hotfix 30.7.2
patchlogs.fixes: Previously, Sentients adapting to your damage types could have led to fights drawing out just a little too long at all levels of gameplay. This change allows it so that you don’t need to rely solely on high Critical Chance / Critical Damage weapons to take them out. Time to break out the Stug!

patchlogs.name: Sisters of Parvos: Hotfix 30.5.4
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed the Kompressa’s disarmed model being the Stug.

patchlogs.name: Derelict Shift: Update 28.3.0
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed the Stug’s alt-fire doing self-damage.

patchlogs.name: Buried Debts: Hotfix 24.5.6
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed the camera momentarily being jerked forward when shooting the Stug at certain angles.

patchlogs.name: Fortuna: Hotfix 24.2.10
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed the Stug's projectiles interacting physically with ragdolls.

patchlogs.name: Shrine of the Eidolon: Update 22.17.3
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed the Stug blowing up in your face when it is used with Mirage’s Hall of Mirrors.

patchlogs.name: Update 18.8.0
patchlogs.changes: •    Tweaked the Stug and Simulor projectiles to properly appear stacked instead of hovering over each other. This also improves the projectiles landing position on objects.

patchlogs.name: Hotfix 18.6.3
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed an issue causing the Stug’s alt-fire to continually repeat the Stug’s firing audio FX.

patchlogs.name: Update 18.6
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed Angstrum, Opticor and Stug displaying incorrect noise levels in the Arsenal.

patchlogs.name: Hotfix 18.4.10 & Tactial Alert: Divine Will
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed the Stug occasionally firing in the wrong direction, too far off to the side.

patchlogs.name: Hotfix 18.4.7
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed the Stug displaying the incorrect trigger type in the Arsenal as per: https://forums.warframe.com/index.php?/topic/568459-u18-bug-report-megathread/page-36#entry6699873

patchlogs.name: Hotfix 17.5.4
patchlogs.fixes:
Fixed the Stug primary fire shooting continuously and not using ammo.
Fixed uncharged Stug shots doing the same damage as fully charged when upgraded with multishot mods.

patchlogs.name: Update 17.2.4
patchlogs.fixes:
Fixed the Stug being unable to destroy cylinders on Sabotage targets.
Fixed the Stug having no Magazine stat in the Arsenal.

patchlogs.name: Hotfix 14.0.5
patchlogs.changes: Performance Improvements for Mirage + Hall of Mirrors and Angstrum, Ogris, Penta, and Mutalist Quanta, and Stug.

patchlogs.name: Update 13.1.0: Amprex
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed an issue with players other than the owner of the weapon not properly hearing partially charged Stug shots.

patchlogs.name: Hotfix 13.0.4
patchlogs.fixes: Tweaked the sound radius of Hydroid's abilities and the Stug.

patchlogs.name: Update 13: Dark Sectors
patchlogs.changes: Improved the performance of the Stug.

patchlogs.name: Hotfix 11.5.3
patchlogs.fixes: Fixed infinite Ammo exploit with Stug.
